commands.review_summary:
The PR bundles private `@openclaw/llm-core` types into generated public plugin SDK declarations and adds build/postpublish checks that fail when public `.d.ts` artifacts reference that private workspace package.

PR surface: Other +50. Total +50 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main routes public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/llm` declarations through a barrel that re-exports private `@openclaw/llm-core`, while the generator still externalizes that bare specifier. I did not run the build because this review is constrained to keep the checkout read-only.

target.title: Fix private llm-core leaks in plugin SDK declarations
target.body:
## Summary
- bundle private @openclaw/llm-core types into flat plugin SDK declaration output instead of leaving the private workspace package as an external specifier
- add build-time and installed-package checks that fail if public plugin SDK .d.ts files reference @openclaw/llm-core

## Why
@openclaw/llm-core is a private workspace package. The beta package declarations referenced it from generated plugin SDK .d.ts chunks, so external TypeScript consumers could not resolve the package.

## Validation
- pnpm build:strict-smoke
- rg -n "@openclaw/llm-core" dist/plugin-sdk/*.d.ts -S (no matches)
- packed openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz, installed it into a fresh consumer project, and ran tsc with skipLibCheck=false importing openclaw/plugin-sdk and openclaw/plugin-sdk/llm
- collectInstalledPluginSdkDeclarationErrors(...) against the installed tarball returned []
target.author: romneyda
